# Position Specification Document

**Role:** Senior Gameplay Engineer #1 (G11)
**Role Family:** Engineering — Gameplay
**Seniority:** Senior (L2)
**Team:** Casual Games Studio — Engineering Division
**Reports To:** Dmitri Volkov (Senior Game Engineer)
**Priority:** P0
**Compensation Band:** $130K–$170K base
**Document ID:** PSD-2026-G11-001

---

## 1. Role Justification

The Senior Gameplay Engineer #1 is a core engineering role responsible for combat systems, progression systems, and AI behavior implementation. This is a P0 hire because Stage 5 (Full Production) requires multiple senior gameplay engineers working in parallel to deliver the game's core player-facing systems on schedule. The role reports to Dmitri Volkov (Senior Game Engineer) and works alongside the second Senior Gameplay Engineer.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 2, 3, 5

---

## 2. Required Competencies (weighted)

| Competency                     | Weight | Minimum Threshold | Assessment Method                |
| ------------------------------ | ------ | ----------------- | -------------------------------- |
| Gameplay Systems Programming   | 25%    | ≥ 4/5             | Coding challenge + system design |
| Combat & Progression Systems   | 20%    | ≥ 4/5             | Case study: design combat loop   |
| AI Behavior Programming        | 15%    | ≥ 3/5             | Technical interview              |
| Unity/Unreal Engine            | 15%    | ≥ 4/5             | Technical deep-dive interview    |
| Mobile Optimization            | 15%    | ≥ 3/5             | Performance profiling exercise   |
| Team Collaboration & Mentoring | 10%    | ≥ 3/5             | Behavioral interview             |

---

## 3. Must-Have Qualifications

- 5+ years gameplay programming experience
- Combat and progression systems design and implementation
- AI behavior programming (state machines, behavior trees, utility AI)
- Unity and/or Unreal Engine proficiency
- Mobile game optimization (CPU, GPU, memory, battery)

---

## 4. Compensation Structure

| Component         | Range / Value                                      |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Base Salary       | $130K–$170K (positioned at 75th percentile: $150K) |
| Performance Bonus | Up to 12% of base                                  |
| Equity            | 0.10%–0.18% (Senior tier)                          |
| Signing Bonus     | Up to $20K (if leaving unvested equity)            |
| Relocation        | Up to $10K                                         |
| Conference Budget | $5K/year                                           |

---

## 5. Sourcing Channels (prioritized)

| Channel                                         | Priority | Quality Weight |
| ----------------------------------------------- | -------- | -------------- |
| Supercell, King, Playrix alumni                 | 1        | 1.5×           |
| Unity Connect / Unreal Marketplace top creators | 2        | 1.3×           |
| GDC Gameplay Track speakers                     | 3        | 1.2×           |
| Referrals from Dmitri Volkov                    | 4        | 1.5×           |
| LinkedIn passive sourcing                       | 5        | 1.0×           |

---

## 6. Assessment Battery

| Assessment                 | Format                      | Duration | Weight |
| -------------------------- | --------------------------- | -------- | ------ |
| Coding Challenge           | 2 problems (gameplay focus) | 90 min   | 25%    |
| System Design              | Combat loop architecture    | 45 min   | 20%    |
| Simulated Panel Interview  | AI panel, signal questions  | 45 min   | 25%    |
| Behavioral / Culture Add   | STAR method                 | 30 min   | 15%    |
| Portfolio / Public Signals | Shipped titles, open source | —        | 15%    |

---

## 7. Success Criteria

| Milestone   | Criteria                                                                         |
| ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Day 1**   | Engine access granted, dev environment running, first code review completed      |
| **Week 1**  | First PR merged; combat system prototype started                                 |
| **Month 1** | Core combat loop implemented and integrated with animation system                |
| **Month 3** | Full progression system shipped to vertical slice; mentoring mid-level engineers |
